reviews.review_description: Have you ever been at your job and thought to yourself how much easier it would be or how super productive things would be if the whole crew was just like you? Well now you can find out if you were right with Unloop because the whole crew is you. I think this is a very fun and unique game in a market filled with shooters and roguelikes (which can also be great too don't get me wrong, Into The Radius may be the best game ever). Last time I played I just stood back and laughed until I almost fell down at the 10 different versions of me talking smack to each other because it all happened organically as I was figuring out the puzzle. Some creative thinking went in to creating these puzzles too. Some can be real head scratchers. CM knocks it out of the park again with Unloop. I am a big fan of the work you do!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for such a great review! 
The team worked really hard to make the game both funny and challenging. We're really glad you're enjoying it  (๑˃ᴗ˂)و

reviews.review_description:
If you’re a fan of puzzle games this is definitly one you should get. It’s like a trial and error type of puzzle game. Not a lot of hard thinking, just you (and a bunch of other yous) trying to solve various time based puzzles. 

Be warned it doesn’t look like they have a teleport option. But you can use the vignette. 

To the developers, if you could add an option for like telekinesis to pick things up like in “I Expect You To Die” that would be great for people!

reviews.review_title: 3.5 stars. Not bad, but could be better
reviews.review_description:
I'm a bit torn. Don't get me wrong, I enjoy the game. The puzzles  are fun and interesting. Yet I keep thinking about ways it could be better. It really boils down to Player Convenience. This game has very little of it. 

If you've played Ratchet and Clank Future: A Crack in Time, then you've seen this concept in the Great Clock puzzles. As a concept, this game is identical. But there are a few things that I can only describe as inconvenient and a bit sloppy. Briefly, the clock is counting down and restarting automatically. You don't decide when the recordings start moving. And there's no limit to the number of recordings that can be overlapping. This, I see as a missed opportunity. Maybe it's just my opinion, but I think it would much cleaner and more engaging if there was a limit, causing you to rewrite certain recordings (going through the same motions as before, and then continuing beyond what was possible in the first recording).

Like I said, it's fun and it makes you think. I'd recommend getting it when it's on sale or if you have a discount.

reviews.review_title: I warned my past self about getting shot
reviews.review_description: The 3rd version of myself decided to shoot random lasers everywhere, what happened next was a clone of me got shot, EVERY SINGLE ONE AFTER THAT WARMED HIM BEFORE IT HAPPENED. i couldn’t save him. it was like 8 dudes saying “YO LOOK OUT HE’S GONNA SHOOT YOU!!!” Kaboom. 11/10 would recommend. Made my future self laugh.

The later levels glitch and the frame rate drops to 50 FPS, or lower. I can only assume that this was never tested on a Quest 2.
And yes I did just spend an hour trying to force the FPS, CPU, texture size etc. A 512 texture size boosts the FPS to 80 but then nothing in game is readable.
Really fun game its so cool how your teammates are you and it copies your movements and you're talking it's really cool but if you're contemplating buying it just buy it

developer_response.body:
Yay! Thanks for you warm words!

Have fun ＼(≧▽≦)／

reviews.review_description: No words except for, wow. One of the most unique games i have ever played in my life. I love watching my clones do my dirty work for me. Its like a dream come true. Great job Devs!

developer_response.body: Thanks, these are exactly the feelings and emotions we wanted players to experience! Have fun  ଘ(੭ˊᵕˋ)੭* ੈ✩‧₊˚

reviews.review_description: Fun asf

reviews.review_title: Oh my gosh this game sucks
reviews.review_description: I just bought this game and it first of all I had to turn on adjust height just to play the game for no reason. And I hate it so much because you’re missing the one thing that makes a game good, A TUTORIAL. This game is terrible and if you want a good game don’t look here

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for trying UnLoop, and we’re really sorry you didn’t enjoy our gameplay.

Calibration is needed so that your past copies can correctly replicate your movements, including squats and bends.

The lack of tutorial is intentional, so that the player can truthfully feel like a spy on a secret mission. In our vision, this fits better into the narrative and enhances immersion, fostering a spirit of discovery, investigation and experimentation.

We’ll consider how we might integrate a tutorial in a way that doesn’t contradict our hero’s nature and allows players of all backgrounds to enjoy UnLoop.

Thanks again for giving the game a chance!

reviews.review_description: Use code bam for 10% off
Loop
Easy 5/5 stars

After a bit of an adjustment period (I'm coming from Last Clockwinder), it clicked. The game is actually from the developers of Into the Radius (but a completely different genre), yet they prove here too that they know what they're doing.

Occasionally, but rarely, an action might fail because the clone, for example, doesn't catch something properly or doesn't insert a battery. This is within a tolerable range.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for your review, we really appreciate it! 

We’re actively working to improve replay accuracy, but occasional desynchronization still happens. 
Team is working hard to resolve these “temporary anomalies, we apologize for these issues and hope that the UnLoop pros will outweigh the cons for the other players too!

Thanks (b ᵔ▽ᵔ)b

this is the best vr puzzle game I've ever played get this game

reviews.review_title: Very fun and tricky! also funny!
reviews.review_description: Its super fun
This is a great game to relax and have fun its a defenite try.  have fun arguing with your self
This is very fun

If you're a fan of puzzle games like Portal, you're going to love this.
Not only will you be using you, yourself and other yous, you'll get to communicate with your future/past selves whilst you do it to coordinate your efforts in solving the puzzles.

Great puzzles, great mechanics, great game!

developer_response.body:
Thanks! 
We're very happy to hear that you enjoyed the game! Fun fact: our level and narrative designers are experienced modders from the Portal community.

Creating interesting puzzles with time loops is a huge challenge, and we're thrilled that we managed to make them interesting and engaging!

reviews.review_description: I like arguing with myself

reviews.review_title: Great idea a bit too tedious
reviews.review_description: Really cool concept, worth pkaying. But for me the room to room puzzle difficulty was super variant. I really felt the want for a speed up button too which other similar games have.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, agent! We understand you’d like a speed-up button for puzzles so you don’t have to wait while the copies do their job. It’s a great idea, though the chances of us adding it are quite low at this stage of development and the game’s lifecycle. Maybe VR also adds some extra psychological pressure during waiting – so here's that. 

Thanks again for your feedback and for giving the game a try – it really means a lot to us.

reviews.review_description: M

developer_response.body: Hello there! If you faced any issues in the game or have questions regarding puzzles, feel free to join our Discord and ask there https://discord.gg/HQfWKaXqx6

reviews.review_description:
Such a wonderful game, but warning, if you are elderly, physically impaired or wheelchair bound, there is no crouch option, for a game where you have to pick things up off the floor it is a terrible shame that those of us who aren't fully able bodied are not considered.
There seems to an issue when setting the turning to smooth turn, as the right controller then becomes very unresponsive.
All the above aside it runs very well and the mechanics/puzzles are exceptionally well implemented.
The game gives me an 'I Expect You To Die' meets 'Portal' feel (although there are no portals).
If a crouch option could be added and the turning issue solved this would be a guaranteed 5 star game.

developer_response.body:
Hi! We've added Seated Mode as an accessibility option in 1.0.1, available via the "Access Configuration" tab of the main menu. 
With this mode active, use the X/A button on your controller to toggle crouching.

reviews.review_description: I have played a lot of clone games on Quest and PSVR but this is by far the best. The game itself is fun and tricky, but having 20, 30, 40 or even 50 copies of yourself running around helping, shooting, taking and getting each others way is one of the highlights of all my many years in VR.  Highy  recommended for all VR players and a great game for first timers to see something that can ONLY be done in VR.

I absolutely love this game. Once I first started, I found it very difficult to stop playing. It’s one of those “just one more loop” kind of game. I love the puzzle aspect and find myself giggling at my previous selves’ chatter (and being able to smack them lol). I struggled with throwing objects a lot but the game is still worthy of five stars. Very clever puzzle game! Highly recommended!

I have loved every game these guys have made and this is just as good I do think that the ‘clones’ sometimes just failing to do things later as you are playing is super annoying but I love to just have like 50 clones doing something stupid so 5 stars a very refreshing experience would recommend

I like the game. I like the idea of the game. There is one thing though. This could be me just being picky but I feel like the player animations needs a bit of polish. I'm not calling it ugly or anything but, for example, when you raise your hands and look at the clone, its shoulder is clipping and your running animation is unchanged no matter what direction you move. As well as the ragdoll physics for when a clone dies is just... unsatisfying. I don't see anyone talking about this so this is probably me being picky. The game is new too so... Sorry to the Devolpers if this too picky.

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thanks for your review. We think it’s good to be picky because there’s always room to improve (whatever we talk about). With UnLoop, we prioritise gameplay consistency, especially making sure clones repeat actions correctly. That’s why some things like clipping shoulders occur or item physics might look simple or less polished.

We’re working on improving physics and adding new puzzles in the next big update, so that's something you can expect! We hope the points you mentioned don’t take away from your fun playing the game. Thanks again for your feedback.

reviews.review_title: Great Game with some minor bugs
reviews.review_description:
I love this game, but even before the last update, it **crashed on me pretty often**. I was really looking forward to the new update because I enjoy playing **seated**, but I need to point out two things right away: 1.  When you set the "seated" mode, the **clone can only "kill itself" when crouching**. When standing up, no matter where you aim the gun, the shot doesn't kill the clone;  2.  **Constant crashing.** I can't finish a single mission without the game crashing. It's a real shame because the devs were genius in creating a game that looks difficult but is actually accessible to everyone with a little bit of thinking (never underestimate a player's ego), and solving the levels is **hugely satisfying**.

EDIT

An extra star compared to my original review because with the help of the devs' response, I solved the crashing issue (which was on my end, not the game's).
Regarding the clone not being able to kill itself, I think there was a misunderstanding. I'm a clone, I finish my task, and I want to self-destruct so I don't lose time and can proceed with another clone. In seated mode, even if I point the gun at my head, the shot doesn't kill me. If I crouch and try again, the shot does kill me. I hope I've been clearer this time, as I don't think this is an intended game mechanic.

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks for writing us, and really apologize for your frustrating experience.

We double-checked on our end and here's what we have:

1. Killing copies in sitting mode works both standing and sitting. 
However, if you bend forward much and keep your arms close to your body, your arm collides with your virtual body and the shot is not counted (to prevent self-killing). 
We have an idea how to fix it and will try to tune it in the next patch. 
For now, try to keep your arms slightly forward when shooting. Let us know if it works as a workaround for your issue. 

2. As for crashes. First, try to clear game's data (visit headset settings – general – storage – find ITR in the list and clear app's data, it's a button on the left side from uninstall option). This will reset your progress (!!!!) and all in-game settings. 

If this didn't help and the game keeps crashing – please email us at unloop@cm.games with additional details about your device – model and OS, we'll try to find the cause and fix if possible.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

reviews.review_title: A fun and addictive puzzle game!
reviews.review_description:
UnLoop is refreshingly addictive — a clever twist on puzzle-solving where your best teammate is… your past self. The timeloop mechanic feels brilliant, the retro sci-fi world is gorgeous, and it’s one of those games where you just want ‘one more loop.
I'm excited to keep going. Ok, some of the puzzles are head scratchers lol... but it's really hard to stop playing. I love the challenge.
I played this for about 30 minutes and I am hooked! Only thing that stopped me from playing more was the battery running on it. Jumping back in as soon as it's charged!

I will change this review to 5 stars once this has been fixed because its a little game breaking for me

The idea art style and all the levels that did work are top notch. And i highly recommend this game. With that said.


The physics in this game can be buggy which causes certain thrown objects, when thrown by the clone (This happened most with the batteries) to just seemingly randomly just go to a different spot to where it was originally thrown to. 

Which means that it’s entirely just messed up your chain of actions.

I was really really enjoying this game until i had to repeat a level like 7 - 8 times becuase i would record myself throwing and catching the battery just for the clones to decide they want to throw it to a completely new location, and then if i decide to then, record from the new location the clone will then throw to the original spot and now ive got two clones interacting with the same key port and messing it up

THIS MUST BE FIXED IF YOU WANT THIS GAME TO WORK.

developer_response.body:
Hello!

Thank you for playing UnLoop.

You can end (kill) the clone that made the mistake, but yes, if you miss something several times in a row, you’ll have a bunch of clones to deal with. We agree this can mess up the flow a bit.

Unfortunately, our physics system is pretty foundational to the game, and we can’t change it too much without impacting everything else. Your feedback is noted though, it's just that we can't willingly in an instant change throwing experience for example or how battery reacts when it lands after a throw.

reviews.review_description: Ever time a clone throws a card it has a good chance to just disappear mid air so then the next clone that’s so post to grab it is walking around with nothing so counter in that you sometime replay the throwing part over and over while finishing the rest of the puzzle this happens a lot

developer_response.body:
Hi! 

Could you please share more details with us on our Discord https://discord.gg/HQfWKaXqx6 , or Reddit /r/unloop or email Kostya (kostya@cm.games) about this issue? Screen recording would help. Clones desync is indeed can occur in rare cases, but we haven't seen many cases of card disappearing mid-air, so that's why we're asking for additional info from you. Thank you in any case.

reviews.review_title: Clever Time Puzzle from The Radius Team!
reviews.review_description:
UnLoop comes from the acclaimed CM Games studio — the creators of Into The Radius 1 & 2, both magnificent and thrilling adventure games. This time, the team breaks away from their adventure roots to explore the puzzle genre, and they do it with remarkable success.

From the very start, you can feel the polish of a big studio: smooth performance, intuitive design, and instantly engaging gameplay. The temporal loop mechanic is brilliantly crafted — you’ll find yourself experimenting with different solutions again and again, not out of frustration but because it genuinely sparks your problem-solving instincts. It lights up your frontal lobe in the best way, making each breakthrough deeply satisfying.

What makes UnLoop stand out is that, although it’s a solo game, it manages to create the sensation of playing alongside multiple versions of yourself (play it, and you’ll understand exactly what that means). It’s a clever and original concept, executed flawlessly.

Bravo again to CM Games for delivering another fantastic title to the Meta Store. Highly recommended for puzzle fans and anyone looking for a fresh VR experience.
